name: blue-hydra
title: blue-hydra

commands.name: blue_hydra

arguments.description: Suppress output and run in daemon mode
arguments.raw: -d, --daemonize                  Suppress output and run in daemon mode

arguments.description: Hide mac addresses in CLI UI
arguments.raw: -z, --demo                       Hide mac addresses in CLI UI

arguments.description: Store results in a file for review
arguments.raw: --pulse-debug                Store results in a file for review

arguments.description: Open 127.0.0.1:1124 to allow other processes to poll for seen devices and rssi
arguments.raw: --rssi-api                   Open 127.0.0.1:1124 to allow other processes to poll for seen devices and rssi

arguments.description: For the purposes for fox hunting, don't info scan.  Some info may be missing, but there will be less gaps during tracking
arguments.raw: --no-info                    For the purposes for fox hunting, don't info scan.  Some info may be missing, but there will be less gaps during tracking

arguments.description: For the purposes of making a hat to cover a mohawk, shit out the ui as json at /dev/shm/blue_hydra.json
arguments.raw: --mohawk-api                 For the purposes of making a hat to cover a mohawk, shit out the ui as json at /dev/shm/blue_hydra.json

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# blue_hydra -h
 Usage: BlueHydra [options]
   -d, --daemonize                  Suppress output and run in daemon mode
   -z, --demo                       Hide mac addresses in CLI UI
   -p, --pulse                      Send results to hermes
       --pulse-debug                Store results in a file for review
       --no-db                      Keep db in ram only
       --rssi-api                   Open 127.0.0.1:1124 to allow other processes to poll for seen devices and rssi
       --no-info                    For the purposes for fox hunting, don't info scan.  Some info may be missing, but there will be less gaps during tracking
       --mohawk-api                 For the purposes of making a hat to cover a mohawk, shit out the ui as json at /dev/shm/blue_hydra.json
   -v, --version                    Show version and quit
   -h, --help                       Show this message

local_help.--help:
Usage: BlueHydra [options]
    -d, --daemonize                  Suppress output and run in daemon mode
    -z, --demo                       Hide mac addresses in CLI UI
    -p, --pulse                      Send results to hermes

        --no-db                      Keep db in ram only
        --rssi-api                   Open 127.0.0.1:1124 to allow other processes to poll for seen devices and rssi
        --no-info                    For the purposes for fox hunting, don't info scan.  Some info may be missing, but there will be less gaps during tracking
        --mohawk-api                 For the purposes of making a hat to cover a mohawk, shit out the ui as json at /dev/shm/blue_hydra.json
    -v, --version                    Show version and quit
    -h, --help                       Show this message
local_help.-h:
Usage: BlueHydra [options]
    -d, --daemonize                  Suppress output and run in daemon mode
    -z, --demo                       Hide mac addresses in CLI UI
    -p, --pulse                      Send results to hermes
        --pulse-debug                Store results in a file for review
        --no-db                      Keep db in ram only
        --rssi-api                   Open 127.0.0.1:1124 to allow other processes to poll for seen devices and rssi
        --no-info                    For the purposes for fox hunting, don't info scan.  Some info may be missing, but there will be less gaps during tracking
        --mohawk-api                 For the purposes of making a hat to cover a mohawk, shit out the ui as json at /dev/shm/blue_hydra.json
    -v, --version                    Show version and quit
    -h, --help                       Show this message
local_help.help:
data_objects, part of datamapper, is not compatible with your version of ruby
A patch is availble here: https://pentoo.org/~zero/data_objects-fixnum2integer.patch

binary_metadata.file_type: POSIX shell script, ASCII text executable

apt_metadata.depends: bluez-test-scripts, python3, ruby (>= 1:3.3), ruby (<< 1:3.4), libc6 (>= 2.38), libruby3.3 (>= 3.3.0~preview1), libsqlite3-0 (>= 3.5.9)
apt_metadata.description:
Bluetooth device discovery service
BlueHydra is a Bluetooth device discovery service built on top of
the bluez library. BlueHydra makes use of ubertooth where available
and attempts to track both classic and low energy (LE) bluetooth devices
over time.

use_cases: Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Indexing only — no automated invocation. The operator must manually inspect the repository before adding to a toolchain.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.

Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Pair with the LLM system-prompt stanza to ensure the chain planner knows how to call this tool.
